name: RuView
tagline: Your WiFi router as a motion sensor
description: A WiFi-sensing platform that turns Channel State Information from cheap ESP32 nodes into presence detection, activity recognition, and claimed vital-sign monitoring — no cameras, no wearables. Integrates with Home Assistant, Apple Home, Google Home, and Alexa, with hardware starting around $8.
why: Camera-free ambient sensing is a genuinely hot privacy-versus-surveillance frontier, and this makes it accessible on $8 hardware. It's also a case study in reading extraordinary claims critically.
audience: Home Assistant tinkerers, IoT researchers, privacy-conscious builders

setup.requirements: ESP32 hardware ($8-140), a host machine, and per-room calibration

limitations: Accuracy is highly environment-dependent, the through-wall vital-sign claims are not independently validated, and monitoring people without consent raises real ethical issues. We feature it with those caveats front and center.
take: Delightful and unsettling in equal measure. Included because it's genuinely novel — and flagged because novelty isn't the same as validation.
